skill.name: quality-documentation-manager
skill.description: Provides structured guidance for quality document control, regulatory documentation review, change control, and document management system planning for medical device organizations.
skill.summary: Plan and review regulated quality documentation workflows.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static weak cryptography, Windows SAM, network reconnaissance, and shell execution alerts to be false positives in Markdown prose, code fences, and inline file names. No executable command runner, credential access, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed files.

content.value_statement: Regulated teams need consistent document control across reviews, approvals, changes, and retention. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structure quality documentation workflows for medical device compliance.
actual_capabilities: Defines document control system components for classification, approval, distribution, maintenance, and retention.
Guides regulatory documentation review for FDA, EU MDR, ISO, and international market submissions.
Outlines change control and configuration management workflows for controlled document updates.
Supports planning for electronic document management systems and 21 CFR Part 11 controls.
Provides documentation quality metrics, audit focus areas, and continuous improvement practices.
limitations: Does not include working automation scripts beyond a placeholder example script.
References and assets are placeholders, not complete implementation materials.
Does not replace qualified regulatory, legal, or quality system approval.
Requires user-provided procedures, product context, and jurisdiction details for precise recommendations.

use_cases.title: Design a Document Control Process
use_cases.description: Create a structured process for document classification, review, approval, distribution, revision control, retention, and disposal.

use_cases.title: Review Regulatory Documentation
use_cases.description: Check whether submission documents are organized, traceable, and aligned with FDA, EU MDR, ISO, or market-specific expectations.

use_cases.title: Plan DMS Implementation
use_cases.description: Define document management system requirements, validation needs, electronic signature controls, and rollout activities.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Document Control Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a practical document control checklist for our quality team. Include classification, approvals, version control, distribution, retention, and obsolete document handling.

prompt_templates.title: Assess a Change Control Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this document change control workflow and identify missing steps, approval controls, training needs, and closure evidence. Use ISO 13485 expectations where relevant.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Regulatory Document Oversight
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a regulatory documentation oversight plan for a medical device submission. Address FDA, EU MDR, ISO standards, traceability, document quality checks, and response management.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Validated DMS Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a DMS implementation strategy for a regulated medical device company. Include requirements, workflow design, access controls, validation, 21 CFR Part 11 controls, and rollout governance.

output_examples.input: A quality manager asks for a new document control procedure outline.
output_examples.output: A structured procedure outline covering document types, numbering, authorship, review, approval, release, access control, periodic review, archiving, and secure disposal.

output_examples.input: A regulatory affairs lead asks for a submission document readiness review.
output_examples.output: A review plan that checks completeness, regulatory alignment, cross-references, traceability, version status, reviewer accountability, and open documentation gaps.

output_examples.input: A quality systems lead asks for DMS implementation planning support.
output_examples.output: A phased implementation plan covering requirements, vendor selection, configuration, validation, user training, change management, and performance monitoring.

best_practices: Provide the current procedure, target regulation, document type, and approval workflow before asking for review.
Ask for traceability checks when documents connect to risk management, design control, clinical, or submission records.
Treat outputs as review support and route final decisions through authorized quality and regulatory approvers.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as the sole authority for regulatory submission approval.
Do not request generic templates without naming the document type, jurisdiction, and intended process owner.
Do not assume placeholder references or assets contain complete operational procedures.

faq.question: What type of documentation does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports quality system documents, regulatory submission documents, change control records, DMS planning artifacts, and audit preparation materials.

faq.question: Is this skill only for medical device teams?
faq.answer: The guidance is written for medical device organizations, but many document control concepts apply to other regulated environments.

faq.question: Can it create compliant final procedures?
faq.answer: It can draft and review procedure structures, but authorized quality and regulatory staff must approve final controlled documents.

faq.question: Does it include working automation tools?
faq.answer: No. The package includes a placeholder script only, so automation must be implemented separately.

faq.question: Which standards are referenced?
faq.answer: The skill references ISO 13485, ISO 14971, EU MDR, FDA submission pathways, 21 CFR Part 11, and related documentation expectations.

faq.question: What input improves the results?
faq.answer: Provide the document purpose, current workflow, applicable regulations, product type, reviewer roles, and known audit findings.
